expect: Prerequisite-shadow concept >=2x DECAYED ratio. B8 retest CONFIRMED.
actual: Prerequisite-shadow CONFIRMED at 7.32x (threshold 2.0x). Group A (>=3 EXPIRED/DECAYED citations): 290 lessons, 80.3% DECAYED. Group B (all citations ACTIVE): 164 lessons, 11.0% DECAYED. Ratio 7.32x. Truly EXPIRED (compacted files): 43/871 cited lessons (4.9%). B8 CONFIRMED: 121 open frontiers (117 domain + 4 global). Pool exhaustion prediction from S464 falsified — domain-level frontier generation compensated. frontier_replenish.py provides structural countermeasure.
diff: Expected >=2x: GOT 7.32x — effect 3.66x stronger than predicted. The prerequisite-shadow is not just real but dominant: 80% of lessons with decayed citation chains are themselves decayed vs 11% baseline. This is the strongest concept-inventor finding to date. B8 expected CONFIRMED: GOT CONFIRMED — upgraded from WEAKENED. Frontier pool grew from ~12 global to 121 total via domain-level expansion.

prerequisite_shadow.confounders: Age correlation — lessons with decayed citations are likely older themselves. Partial confounder: the citation decay IS the mechanism (prerequisite chains decay together), so age is not independent of the effect.

b8_retest.falsification_criterion_1: NOT MET — frontiers continuously generated at domain level

status_change: prerequisite-shadow concept: TESTED → CONFIRMED. B8: WEAKENED → CONFIRMED.
